ambiguities.description: Item model (SKUs, variants, bundles, assemblies, units of measure)
Location model (warehouses, bins, stores, zones, virtual locations)
Stock movements (receipts, transfers, adjustments, consumption, returns)
Stock levels and tracking (on-hand, reserved, available, in-transit, thresholds)
Costing method policy (standard, actual, average — not assumed by default)
Replenishment model (reorder points, min/max, auto-purchase, demand-driven)
Integration with purchasing, sales, manufacturing, or accounting systems
Auditing and traceability (lot numbers, serial numbers, expiry, batch tracking)
Cycle counts and physical inventory reconciliation
Reporting (stock valuation, movement history, aging, shrinkage)
MVP inventory boundary — what item types and locations are in scope

questions.question: What item types must be tracked (SKUs, variants, bundles, assemblies)?
What locations must be tracked (warehouses, stores, bins, zones)?
What stock movement types are required (receipts, transfers, adjustments, returns)?
Must this integrate with purchasing, sales, or accounting systems?
What reporting and analytics are required?

assumptions.assumption: Simple SKU-based items are the core MVP item type
A single warehouse or store location is acceptable for MVP
Basic stock level tracking (on-hand, available) is sufficient initially
Manual replenishment with reorder point alerts is acceptable for MVP

suggested_closures.decision: Inventory construction will start with SKU-based items, single-location tracking, and manual replenishment under an explicit costing policy.
suggested_closures.rationale: A focused item model and conservative location scope reduce risk while preserving principal control over costing and replenishment decisions.

seed_tasks.title: Define item model and SKU structure
seed_tasks.transformation: Document item types, SKU format, variants, bundles, and units of measure.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every item type has a defined structure and SKU format.

seed_tasks.title: Define location model
seed_tasks.transformation: Document warehouses, stores, bins, zones, and how locations relate.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every location has a defined purpose and hierarchy.

seed_tasks.title: Define stock movement types and rules
seed_tasks.transformation: Document receipts, transfers, adjustments, consumption, and returns with authorization rules.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every movement type has an authorized actor and a documented rule.

seed_tasks.title: Define costing method policy
seed_tasks.transformation: Document how costs are assigned and what method is used.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Costing method is documented and every stakeholder understands how valuation is calculated.

seed_tasks.title: Define replenishment and threshold rules
seed_tasks.transformation: Document reorder points, min/max levels, and who triggers replenishment.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every item has a documented reorder rule or an explicit exception.

seed_tasks.title: Define MVP inventory boundary
seed_tasks.transformation: Explicitly list item types, locations, and features in MVP and what is out of scope.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: MVP scope document exists with explicit inclusions and exclusions.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Scope is achievable and every excluded item type is consciously deferred.

residuals.description: Item model and SKU structure are not defined.
Stock movement types and rules are not decided.
